name: El Chorro de la Meancera waterfall from El Gasco
name_original: Cascada El Chorro de la Meancera desde El Gasco
region_intro: Hiking around Nuñomoral offers access to diverse natural landscapes within the Las Hurdes region of Extremadura. The area is characterized by lush forests, winding rivers, and numerous waterfalls, including the Chorro de las Batuecas and Chorro de la Meancera. Hikers will encounter mountainous terrain and river valleys, with natural pools carved into rock formations. Best hiking trails around Nuñomoral The most popular hiking route is Valle de las Batuecas to Chorro de las Batuecas Trail, a 6.4 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 0 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a river through forests to a 12-meter waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Chorro de la Meancera waterfall from El Gasco, an easy 1.6 miles (2.6 km) path. This shorter trail leads directly to the Chorro de la Meancera waterfall. Local hikers also love the Majá Robledo Trail, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Nuñomoral is defined by river valleys, dense forests, and impressive waterfalls, offering routes for both easy walks and more challenging moderate excursions. The region's network of trails provides options for different ability levels. The routes in Nuñomoral are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Nuñomoral's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Nuñomoral?
A: Nuñomoral offers a diverse range of hiking opportunities, with over 19 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy walks to more moderate excursions through the region's unique landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Nuñomoral has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990362286/el-chorro-de-la-meancera-waterfall-from-el-gasco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro de la Meancera waterfall from El Gasco&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 1.6-mile path that leads directly to a beautiful waterfall. There are 8 easy routes in total, providing accessible options for everyone.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls around Nuñomoral?
A: Absolutely. Waterfalls are a prominent feature of the landscape around Nuñomoral. You can hike the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993035340/valle-de-las-batuecas-to-chorro-de-las-batuecas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de las Batuecas to Chorro de las Batuecas Trail&lt;/a&gt; to reach the impressive 12-meter Chorro de las Batuecas waterfall. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990362286/el-chorro-de-la-meancera-waterfall-from-el-gasco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro de la Meancera waterfall from El Gasco&lt;/a&gt;, leading to the Chorro de la Meancera. The region is rich with these natural attractions.

Q: Are there any trails with great viewpoints in the area?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Nuñomoral offers several stunning viewpoints. While hiking, you might encounter vistas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4386882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Blanco Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The broader region is known for miradores such as Mirador de la antigua, La Antigua Lookout, Mirador De Las Carrascas, and Mirador del Gasco, providing breathtaking panoramas of the diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Nuñomoral?
A: Hiking in Nuñomoral immerses you in a wild and preserved environment. You'll encounter lush forests, winding rivers, and impressive waterfalls. The terrain often features river valleys and mountainous areas, with unique natural pools carved into rock formations by water erosion. It's part of a region known for its 'almost untouched natural areas' and 'spectacular scenery'.

Q: Are there any natural pools or places to swim along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the region is famous for its natural pools. You can find refreshing spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5579125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Olla Natural Pool, Las Mestas&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6968437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt;. The Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve, nearby, also features the famous 'Los Pilones' – natural pools perfect for a dip after a hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Nuñomoral?
A: The best time for hiking in Nuñomoral is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, making it ideal for exploring the trails and enjoying the natural beauty without the extreme heat of summer or the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

Q: Is Nuñomoral a good destination for hiking with dogs?
A: Nuñomoral's natural environment is generally welcoming for hikers with dogs. Many trails wind through forests and along rivers, offering plenty of space for your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific trails or natural parks you plan to visit.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Nuñomoral?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead are not always explicitly marked, you can typically find parking in or near the villages that serve as starting points for the trails, such as El Gasco for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990362286/el-chorro-de-la-meancera-waterfall-from-el-gasco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro de la Meancera waterfall from El Gasco&lt;/a&gt; route. Look for designated parking lots or suitable roadside spots in these areas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Nuñomoral?
A: The trails in Nuñomoral are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the area for its stunning natural beauty, including the impressive waterfalls and lush forests, as well as the variety of routes suitable for different abilities. Over 600 hikers have used komoot to explore this region.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers looking for a challenge, Nuñomoral offers several moderate routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990859934/viewpoint-of-pico-blanco-from-la-pesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Pico Blanco from La Pesga&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.4-mile trail with significant elevation gain, offering rewarding views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990861900/tapias-dam-trail-from-la-pesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tapias Dam Trail from La Pesga&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.7-mile route.

Q: Can I find accommodation or places to eat near the hiking areas?
A: Nuñomoral and the surrounding villages in the Las Hurdes region offer various options for accommodation, including rural guesthouses and small hotels. You'll also find local restaurants and cafes where you can enjoy traditional Extremaduran cuisine after your hike. These establishments are typically concentrated in the main villages, providing convenient access for hikers.
tips:
The Chorro de la Meancera is an impressive waterfall located in the hamlet of El Gasco, in the region of Las Hurdes, Cáceres. This waterfall is one of the highest and most aesthetic in the region, with a drop of approximately 100 meters.
The best time to visit the Chorro de la Meancera is from autumn to spring, when the water flow is greater due to the rains. In summer, the flow may be lower, but the beauty of the place is still impressive.
